decision: Surface real connection credentials for known testcontainer images via an explicit env-var allowlist on the single-container inspect, rendered behind a reveal toggle in ContainerCard
rationale: The MySQL Connection panel showed hardcoded "[REDACTED]" string literals that were never real masked values; the password never left the server, the copy button produced a non-working connection string, and user/database were already shown in clear, making password-only hiding incoherent. These are ephemeral throwaway test creds on a single-user trusted Tailscale network.
alternatives: (a) Hardcode the template default "test" client-side: rejected, wrong for any container with customized env and still leaves copy string broken in custom cases; (b) Heavyweight authenticated reveal endpoint with audit logging: rejected as over-engineered for the threat model.

decision: Keep the existing sensitive-key stripping in DockerClient.getContainer (environmentKeys) and the bulk list untouched; add a separate connectionCredentials field populated only on single-container inspect
rationale: Bulk listings and the general env-key view must stay redacted; only an explicit allowlist of connection-relevant vars (DB user/password/db/root password, AMQP, Keycloak admin) is surfaced, and only on an explicit per-container fetch.
alternatives: Enriching the bulk list with N inspect calls so secrets are present everywhere: rejected for latency and for spreading secrets into bulk responses.

decision: Gate connectionCredentials behind an explicit getContainer({includeCredentials:true}) option used only by GET /api/testcontainers/[id]; URL-encode user/password in URI connection strings
rationale: Cross-provider (Codex/GPT-5) validation flagged that getContainer is also called by create/start/stop/restart responses (broadening secret exposure) and that un-encoded credentials produce broken URIs for passwords containing reserved chars. Gating narrows secret exposure to the single detail fetch; encodeURIComponent keeps the copyable connection string valid while the credentials grid still shows raw values.
alternatives: Leaving credentials on every getContainer response (rejected: wider surface than intended); not encoding (rejected: copy string breaks for special-char passwords — the original bug being fixed).

decision: Accept two Codex findings as intentional: copy-on-masked returns the real value (password-manager pattern), and GET /api/testcontainers/[id] stays unauthenticated
rationale: Copying the working secret while the screen shows dots is the desired UX (shoulder-surfing safe, clipboard usable). The detail/list reads are already public under the single-user trusted-Tailscale deployment model; only mutations require auth. These are ephemeral throwaway test creds.
alternatives: Require reveal before copy (rejected: friction, defeats the fix); add auth to GET (rejected: inconsistent with existing public list/detail and would break the reveal fetch).
